Description: Initial .bas files had LF-only line endings instead of CRLF
RootCause: Write tool on Windows/bash outputs LF by default; VBA module import requires CRLF
Fix: Added sed pass to convert all .bas files to CRLF after writing

Description: Config sheet column indices used hardcoded numbers (1-9) instead of named constants
RootCause: Build prompt did not define constants for config-sheet layout columns; coder used numeric literals
Fix: Added CREG_COL_*, ISCH_COL_*, GCFG_COL_*, TREG_COL_* constants to KernelConstants.bas and updated all readers

Description: Summary tab used magic number 2 for metric column and 2+prd for data columns
RootCause: Summary layout positions not defined as constants in original KernelConstants
Fix: Added SUMMARY_COL_ENTITY, SUMMARY_COL_METRIC, SUMMARY_DATA_START_COL constants; updated KernelOutput.bas

Description: Setup.ps1 hung indefinitely at BootstrapWorkbook macro call
RootCause: Zombie Excel.exe process from prior failed run held COM lock; hidden Excel ($excel.Visible=$false) made error dialogs invisible
Fix: 1) Added zombie Excel process cleanup at script start. 2) Changed Excel to Visible=$true during bootstrap so dialogs are not hidden. 3) Hide Excel again after success.

Description: GenerateInputsTab crashed with Error 1004 writing section header '=== ATTRIBUTES ==='
RootCause: String starting with '=' written via .Value caused Excel to interpret it as a formula. Existing AP-07 guard in WriteCsvLineToRow did not cover GenerateInputsTab which wrote headers directly.
Fix: Added NumberFormat="@" (text format) before writing section header strings starting with '==='. Same pattern already used in LoadCsvToConfigSheet for marker rows.

Description: Bootstrap crash with blank config: Subscript out of range (Error 9) at GenerateInputsTab >> GetSheet
RootCause: CreateTabsFromRegistry relied entirely on tab_registry.csv data rows to create tabs. Blank config has headers only, so no tabs were created. GenerateInputsTab then failed accessing nonexistent Inputs sheet.
Fix: 1) Added EnsureSheet calls for all required kernel tabs (Inputs, ScenarioMgr, Detail, Summary, ErrorLog) at end of CreateTabsFromRegistry. 2) Added early-exit in SetupDetailHeaders when GetColumnCount()=0.

Description: ResumeFrom call from Immediate Window threw Error 424: Object required
RootCause: VBA Immediate Window executes in the context of the active VBA project. When PERSONAL.XLSB project was active, KernelEngine.ResumeFrom was not found in that project.
Fix: Changed walkthrough to use Application.Run "RDK_Model.xlsm!KernelEngine.ResumeFrom" syntax which explicitly targets the correct workbook regardless of active project.

Description: RunProjections validation failure produced no visible feedback
RootCause: Domain validation caught Units=0 and logged to ErrorLog, but no MsgBox or popup appeared. User had no indication anything happened.
Fix: Added MsgBox calls to RunProjections for validation failure, success, and unhandled errors. Users now get visible feedback in all outcomes.

Description: RunProjections macro missing after Setup (VBA import silently failed)
RootCause: KernelEngine.bas lines 64 and 164 contained Unicode em-dash characters. VBA import silently fails on non-ASCII bytes, preventing entire project from compiling.
Fix: Replaced em-dash characters with ASCII double-dash (--) on both lines.

Description: RunProjections not visible in Alt+F8 macro dialog
RootCause: VBA Optional parameters (even with defaults) hide macros from the Alt+F8 dialog. RunProjections had Optional seed As Long = -1.
Fix: Added parameterless RunProjections() wrapper that delegates to RunProjectionsEx(Optional seed).

Description: Optional parameter default referenced cross-module Public Const
RootCause: 4 functions in KernelCompare.bas used Optional threshold As Double = COMPARE_DEFAULT_THRESHOLD. VBA does not allow cross-module constant references as Optional defaults.
Fix: Replaced COMPARE_DEFAULT_THRESHOLD with literal 0.000001 in all 4 function signatures.

Description: PRNG seed overwritten by RunProjections in AUTO mode
RootCause: InitializePRNG always called AutoSeed when DeterministicMode=AUTO and DefaultSeed=0, even if user had manually set a seed via KernelRandom.InitSeed.
Fix: Added KernelRandom.IsInitialized check in AUTO branch; keeps existing seed if already initialized.

Description: ShowScenarios only showed 1 scenario (ListScenarios/ListSavepoints Dir bug)
RootCause: Nested Dir() calls (checking metadata.json inside a Dir folder loop) reset the outer iteration. Only the first matching folder was found.
Fix: Replaced nested Dir() with FileSystemObject in both ListScenarios and ListSavepoints.

Description: RestoreSavepoint crashed with Subscript out of range
RootCause: Config arrays not loaded when RestoreSavepoint called standalone. GetColumnCount() returned 0, causing ReDim with invalid bounds.
Fix: Added KernelConfig.LoadAllConfig at start of RestoreSavepoint and LoadScenario.

Description: ArchiveSavepoint failed with Path/File access error on re-archive
RootCause: Archive destination already had read-only files from a prior archive. FileCopy cannot overwrite read-only files.
Fix: Added pre-archive cleanup: detect existing archive folder, clear read-only, delete before copying.

Description: Bootstrap.ps1 failed with COM error 80080005 when COM add-ins active
RootCause: COM add-ins (e.g. S&P Capital IQ) interfere with programmatic Excel startup via New-Object -ComObject.
Fix: Launch Excel with /automation flag to suppress add-in loading during bootstrap.

Description: S&P Capital IQ COM add-in permanently broken after Setup.bat
RootCause: Three-link failure chain: (1) Zombie Excel kill (BUG-004 fix) interrupted S&P encrypted storage mid-write, corrupting DPAPI-encrypted IsolatedStorage in %APPDATA%\Roaming\IsolatedStorage. (2) /automation flag launch + $excel.Quit() caused Excel to write incomplete HKCU registry stubs (LoadBehavior=2, no Manifest) that override correct HKLM entries. (3) Failed S&P auto-update (MSI) on 2026-03-21 ran while Excel was open, compounding the issue. The CryptographicException (Padding is invalid) in StorageFolderProvider.GetDictionary prevented add-in initialization.
Fix: 1) Clear corrupted Roaming IsolatedStorage: %APPDATA%\IsolatedStorage\Publisher.rzts3tkwo03sjqp1f4dfa2tfj1uydzrp. 2) Delete HKCU stubs at HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\Excel\Addins\SNL.Clients.Office.Excel.ExcelAddIn and SPGMI.ExcelShell so complete HKLM entries take effect. 3) Updated Bootstrap.ps1 to snapshot/restore HKCU state and delete stubs created by /automation in the finally block. 4) Created Repair-ComAddins.ps1 and Toggle-ComAddins.ps1 scripts.

Description: Domain tests DOM-003 and DOM-004 fail: entities B and C return entity A Revenue
RootCause: SetupTestInputs wrote InputSchema defaults (single value per parameter matching entity A) to ALL entities, overwriting per-entity fixture values set by PopulateSampleData at bootstrap.
Fix: Removed default-writing loop from SetupTestInputs; function now only applies explicit overrides. Added explicit per-entity fixture restore in SampleDomainTests.

Description: RunLint fails with Engine directory not found
RootCause: KernelLint/KernelDiagnostic/KernelHealth used ThisWorkbook.Path & \\engine but workbook lives in workbook/ subdirectory so engine/ is at project root one level up
Fix: Changed all engine path references to ThisWorkbook.Path & \\..\engine in KernelLint, KernelDiagnostic, and KernelHealth

Description: LINT-03 only caught magic numbers in outputs() pattern, not col* = <number>
RootCause: CheckMagicNumbers only looked for outputs(entity, <number>) pattern. Direct column variable assignments like colRevenue = 5 were missed.
Fix: Added MatchesColAssignmentMagic() to detect col<Name> = <number> assignments without ColIndex()

Description: LINT-01 and LINT-12 unreliable for non-ASCII and CRLF detection
RootCause: CheckNonASCII and CheckCRLF operated on StrConv-converted string. StrConv(rawBytes, vbUnicode) codepage conversion could remap or lose byte values.
Fix: Rewrote both checks to scan raw byte array directly (rawBytes(i) > 127 for non-ASCII, byte 10 not preceded by byte 13 for CRLF)

Description: MODULE_SIZE_WARN threshold too high to catch documented 51KB modules
RootCause: MODULE_SIZE_WARN=51200 (50 KiB) but KernelConfig (51130 bytes) and KernelSnapshot (51191 bytes) were 70 and 9 bytes under threshold respectively
Fix: Lowered MODULE_SIZE_WARN from 51200 to 50000 bytes in KernelConstants.bas

Description: MatchesMagicNumberPattern infinite loop when output() exists but pattern does not match
RootCause: Fallback search used InStr(1, ...) hardcoded to position 1 instead of InStr(pos, ...). When output( existed but had no numeric after comma, loop restarted from position 1 forever.
Fix: Changed fallback InStr(1, lnLower, "output(") to InStr(pos, lnLower, "output(") so search advances past each match

Description: KernelDiagnostic ByRef type mismatch in BuildConfigSummary
RootCause: GetFieldClass(cidx) passed Long cidx where GetFieldClass expects metricName As String parameter
Fix: Changed to GetFieldClass(colName) where colName = GetColName(cidx) resolves the name first

Description: Bootstrap.ps1 closes Excel without prompting user to save other workbooks
RootCause: DisplayAlerts=$false set during automation was never restored before $excel.Quit() in finally block. Any other open workbooks with unsaved changes were silently closed.
Fix: Added $excel.DisplayAlerts=$true and $excel.Visible=$true before $excel.Quit() in finally block so Excel shows native save prompts

Description: Exhibits tab used hardcoded computed values instead of formulas
RootCause: GenerateExhibits wrote .Value = val for all data cells. Unlike Summary (SUMIFS), exhibit values were static and did not update when Detail changed.
Fix: Rewrote GenerateExhibits to use SUMIFS formulas for Incremental metrics and cell-reference formulas (e.g., =IFERROR(D5/B5,0)) for Derived metrics. Removed 5 helper functions (LookupDetailValue, RecomputeDerived, FindMetricTotal, FillExhibitDerived, SumEntityMetric), replaced with FillExhDerivedRow and FindExhMetIdx.

Description: ProveIt tab had orphan red cell one row past last data row
RootCause: ApplyProveItFormatting received piRow after a blank-row increment. Empty cell at row N+1 matched FALSE conditional format (empty=0=FALSE in Excel).
Fix: Removed blank-row gap (piRow=piRow+1) before AND summary. AND formula now references E5:E(piRow-1). Formatting range no longer includes empty row.

Description: Chart generation silently skipped charts with invalid metrics (AP-46 violation)
RootCause: GenerateCharts had If metricCol < 1 Then GoTo NextChart with no error logging or user feedback.
Fix: Added chartsSkipped counter, LogError SEV_ERROR E-521 with manual bypass instructions per skipped chart, and consolidated MsgBox showing skip count.

Description: KernelTests.bas LINT-03 false positive on colCount variable
RootCause: Variable name colCount matched lint scanner MatchesColAssignmentMagic() pattern for col* = <number>. colCount = 0 flagged as magic number.
Fix: Renamed colCount to numCols in LoadCsvToArray function (5 locations).

Description: Exhibit tab applied blanket format overriding per-metric formats
RootCause: exhibit_config.csv Format column (#,##0) applied to ALL metric columns including GPMargin which needs 0.0%. Single format per exhibit, not per metric.
Fix: Built per-metric format array (summary_config -> column_registry -> exhibit fallback) and applied per-column NumberFormat at all 4 formatting sites.

Description: config_sample/chart_registry.csv left with FakeMetric from Gate 14 test
RootCause: User edited MetricName to FakeMetric for validation gate testing but change was in config_sample/ source file, not just runtime config/.
Fix: Restored MetricName to Revenue in both config_sample/ and config/ chart_registry.csv.

Description: Pipeline completion MsgBox showed success even with FATAL/ERROR entries logged
RootCause: RunProjections MsgBox always showed vbInformation regardless of errors logged during the run. User had to manually check ErrorLog.
Fix: Added run-level error counters (m_runFatalCount, m_runErrorCount) to KernelConfig.LogError. RunProjections and ResumeFrom reset counters at start, check at end, show vbExclamation with error summary if any FATAL/ERROR logged.

Description: No visible display mode indicator on Dashboard
RootCause: Users could not tell if workbook was in Incremental or Cumulative mode without checking Config sheet or Summary metadata row.
Fix: Added row 2 status cell on Dashboard (color-coded: blue=Incremental, green=Cumulative). Toggle button caption dynamically shows Switch to Incremental/Cumulative. UpdateDashboardMode called from ToggleDisplayMode, RefreshAllPresentations, and bootstrap.

Description: Transform E-700: Cannot run the macro SampleDomainEngine.ApplyLoading
RootCause: Application.Run cannot pass VBA arrays as parameters. RunTransforms passed outputs() to Application.Run which fails with error 1004.
Fix: Added Public TransformOutputs handoff variable. Transforms access KernelTransform.TransformOutputs directly (no parameter). Caller copies in before and out after.

Description: VBA project contained duplicate SampleDomainEngine1 module after Setup
RootCause: SampleDomainTests.bas had incorrect VB_Name=SampleDomainEngine (same as SampleDomainEngine.bas). Both matched SampleDomain* import filter. VBA auto-renamed the second.
Fix: Deleted SampleDomainTests.bas (stale corrupt copy with syntax error on line 131).

Description: Personal.xlsb disabled after running Setup.bat
RootCause: /automation flag suppresses XLSTART loading. On quit Excel may write DisabledItems registry entries preventing XLSTART items from loading next launch.
Fix: Added XLSTART protection to Bootstrap.ps1: snapshot DisabledItems key before launch, remove new entries in finally block.

Description: Named range config RowIDs mismatched AggregateToQuarterly output
RootCause: AggregateToQuarterly generates RowIDs using Left(metricName, 4): Revenue->REVE, GrossProfit->GROS. Config had abbreviated forms QS_REV_TOTAL, QS_GP_TOTAL that did not match.
Fix: Fixed all RowIDs in named_range_registry.csv and formula_tab_config.csv to match generated pattern (QS_REVE_TOTAL, QS_GROS_TOTAL, QS_GPMA_TOTAL).

Description: WriteCsvLineToRow did not force text format on Config sheet cells
RootCause: Excel auto-converted format strings like 0.0% written without NumberFormat=@ to numeric value 0. LoadColumnRegistry then read CStr(0)=0 as format string, causing 0.4 to display as 0.
Fix: Changed WriteCsvLineToRow to ALWAYS set NumberFormat=@ before writing cell values on Config sheet.

Description: WriteQuarterlyHeaders hardcoded row 2 wiped by merge
RootCause: FS_BASIS config entry at row 2 with ColSpan=6 merged A2:F2, overwriting quarterly headers written to row 2.
Fix: Changed WriteQuarterlyHeaders to dynamically find first formula row and write headers one row above, AFTER config processing completes.

Description: Annual total column used SUM for ratio metrics like GP Margin
RootCause: CreateFormulaTabs used =SUM(Q1:Q4) for ALL formulas. Summing percentages (0.4+0.4+0.4+0.4=1.6) is incorrect; ratios must be recomputed from annual totals.
Fix: Changed annual total to use ResolveFormulaPlaceholders with annual column number instead of SUM for all formulas. Ratio formulas resolve correctly (e.g., =IFERROR(GrossProfit/Revenue,0)).

Description: AutoFit reset column Hidden state on formula tabs
RootCause: ws.Columns.AutoFit was called after ws.Columns(FTAB_COL_ROWID).Hidden = True. AutoFit unhid the column.
Fix: Moved .Hidden = True to AFTER .Columns.AutoFit call.

Description: GenerateInputsTab missing number format for Pct and Currency DataTypes
RootCause: COGSPct (DataType=Pct) displayed as 0.6 instead of 60.0%. No format applied to entity input cells based on DataType.
Fix: Added format application in GenerateInputsTab: Pct -> 0.0%, Currency -> #,##0.00, applied to entity input cell range.

Description: RefreshFormulaTabs pipeline call showed MsgBox during RunProjections
RootCause: CreateFormulaTabs and CreateNamedRanges had unconditional MsgBox calls. Pipeline execution paused waiting for user to dismiss dialogs.
Fix: Added m_silent module-level flag. Pipeline callers set m_silent=True. Added RefreshFormulaTabsUI wrapper (m_silent=False) for dashboard button.

Description: Toggle display mode button caption did not update after toggling
RootCause: Button caption set at bootstrap showed current mode but did not change when user toggled display mode.
Fix: Changed button to static caption Toggle Display Mode instead of dynamic state-based label.

Description: Personal.xlsb not loading after running Setup.bat
RootCause: Bootstrap.ps1 launches Excel with /automation flag which suppresses XLSTART loading. On $excel.Quit(), the /automation instance writes shutdown state that can suppress XLSTART items on next normal launch. Two compounding issues: (1) Start-Process PID was discarded so process kill was impossible, (2) Resiliency cleanup only scanned pre-snapshotted paths -- if Resiliency key did not exist before the script, newly created entries were never cleaned up.
Fix: Three fixes: (1) Capture /automation PID from Start-Process, (2) Kill process by PID instead of $excel.Quit() to prevent quit-cycle state writes, (3) Comprehensive post-cleanup scans ALL Office versions for DisabledItems regardless of pre-state.

Description: PDF report cover page produces blank page 2
RootCause: GenerateCoverPage did not set PrintArea, FitToPagesWide/Tall, or Orientation on the _ReportCover sheet. Excel defaulted to printing the full sheet area which spilled onto a second blank page.
Fix: Added PageSetup to GenerateCoverPage: PrintArea constrained to A1:F{lastRow}, FitToPagesWide=1, FitToPagesTall=1, Orientation=Portrait.

Description: Book counter increments across sessions (Book2, Book3...) and stale XLSTART lock file blocks Personal.xlsb
RootCause: Two side effects of killing the /automation Excel process (BUG-045 fix): (1) Process kill leaves orphaned DocumentRecovery registry entries under Resiliency that cause Excel to offset the new workbook counter on next launch. (2) Process kill leaves ~$PERSONAL.XLSB owner/lock file in XLSTART from whatever session originally created it, blocking Personal.xlsb from loading on subsequent launches.
Fix: Added two post-cleanup steps to Bootstrap.ps1 finally block: (1) Clear all DocumentRecovery entries under Resiliency for all Office versions. (2) Remove stale ~$* lock files from XLSTART folder.

Description: PDF report tabs exported with default PageSetup -- no landscape, no fit-to-page, no margins
RootCause: Ext_ReportGen.ExportReportPDF never called KernelPrint.ConfigurePrintSettings before exporting. All tabs went to PDF with Portrait orientation, no fit-to-page, and default margins regardless of print_config.csv settings.
Fix: Added KernelPrint.ConfigurePrintSettings True call in ExportReportPDF before tab selection and export. Added Optional silent parameter to ConfigurePrintSettings to suppress MsgBox when called from pipeline/report paths. ExportPDF and PrintPreview also call with silent=True since they show their own result messages.

Description: Compile error: Variable not defined in RunSmokeTests (CFG_MARKER_GRANULARITY, TAB_ERRORLOG)
RootCause: Tier 5 smoke tests used incorrect constant names: CFG_MARKER_GRANULARITY (actual: CFG_MARKER_GRANULARITY_CONFIG) and TAB_ERRORLOG (actual: TAB_ERROR_LOG). VBA Option Explicit correctly caught these as undefined variables.
Fix: Fixed both references to use the correct constant names from KernelConstants.bas.

Description: Setup.bat fails with Cannot run macro when Excel already running
RootCause: GetActiveObject returns whichever Excel is first in the Running Object Table. If a prior failed Setup left a visible Excel process or the user had Excel open, GetActiveObject connected to that instance instead of the /automation one. The finally block killed only the /automation PID, orphaning the COM-connected instance. On next Setup run, the orphan survived the zombie kill (it had a visible window) and GetActiveObject connected to it again.
Fix: Three fixes: (1) Pre-check after zombie kill -- if any Excel processes still exist, exit with clear message asking user to close Excel. (2) Finally block kills ALL Excel processes (safe because pre-check guarantees none existed before script). (3) Removed S&P warning from zombie kill since headless process kill is low-risk.

Description: Smoke tests SMK-030/031/032 fail -- extension registry not loaded
RootCause: RunSmokeTests called KernelExtension.GetActiveExtensionCount() and IsExtensionActive() but LoadExtensionRegistry was never called. LoadAllConfig does not load the extension registry -- it is managed separately by KernelExtension.
Fix: Added KernelExtension.LoadExtensionRegistry call at the start of RunSmokeTests before extension checks.

Description: RunLint overwrites TestResults headers when run after RunTests
RootCause: WriteLintResults inserted rows at top of TestResults sheet (row 1). This disrupted the merged RDK Test Results title cell at row 1 and pushed all test results down, causing visual corruption of headers.
Fix: Changed WriteLintResults to append at the bottom of existing content instead of inserting at the top. Uses ws.Cells(Rows.Count,1).End(xlUp).Row + 2 to find the append point with a spacer row.

Description: Walkthrough Step 6 tells user to run Setup option [1] after config edit, resetting their change
RootCause: Step 6 (Extension Deactivation) instructed user to edit config_sample/ and re-run Setup option [1]. Users naturally edit config/ instead (the runtime copy). Option [1] deletes config/ and re-copies from config_sample/, silently discarding any config/ edits. Even editing config_sample as instructed is fragile and non-obvious.
Fix: Changed Step 6 to use Setup option [3] (rebuild workbook only, keep config) and edit config/ (the runtime copy). Added callout box warning that option [1] resets config from config_sample.

Description: MsgBox 'Created 1 formula-driven tab' fires during Setup/bootstrap
RootCause: KernelBootstrap step 9d calls KernelFormula.CreateFormulaTabs directly. CreateFormulaTabs uses module-level m_silent flag to gate MsgBox, but m_silent defaults to False. Only RefreshFormulaTabs (pipeline path) set m_silent=True before calling. Bootstrap never set the flag, so the MsgBox fired on every setup.
Fix: Added Optional silent parameter to CreateFormulaTabs and CreateNamedRanges. Bootstrap now calls CreateFormulaTabs True. The silent parameter ORs with m_silent so both pipeline and bootstrap paths are silent. Audited all bootstrap sub-calls -- no other MsgBox paths are reachable.

Description: InsuranceDomainEngine not imported -- RunProjections FATAL E-299 at step 1 with blank error
RootCause: Bootstrap.ps1 line 179 filter only matched Kernel*, SampleDomain*, and Ext_*. InsuranceDomainEngine.bas starts with Insurance so it was never imported into the VBA project. Application.Run InsuranceDomainEngine.Initialize failed with empty Err.Description because the module did not exist.
Fix: Changed Bootstrap.ps1 filter from SampleDomain* to *DomainEngine* so all domain engines (Sample, Insurance, future) are imported. Also matches DomainEngine.bas stub.

Description: UW Inputs tab cleared on every model run -- user loses entered data
RootCause: KernelFormula.RefreshFormulaTabs (called from KernelEngine pipeline steps 4 and 6) calls CreateFormulaTabs which does ws.Cells.ClearContents on every formula tab including UW Inputs. This wiped user-entered premium, loss, and reinsurance inputs on every run.
Fix: Added m_preserveOnRefresh module-level flag to KernelFormula. RefreshFormulaTabs sets flag True before calling CreateFormulaTabs, resets after. CreateFormulaTabs skips ClearContents when flag is True, preserving user input data across runs.

Description: UW Inputs tab stops at row 32 -- Check formula CSV quoting breaks ParseCsvLine
RootCause: Check formulas in formula_tab_config.csv used 3-quote sequences to encode literal double-quotes inside Excel formula strings (Pass/FAIL). ParseCsvLine reads 3-quote as: escaped-quote + field-terminator. Content truncated to partial formula, subsequent fields misaligned, NumberFormat set from garbage (E-800), ErrHandler aborted CreateFormulaTabs so all rows after 32 never written.
Fix: Changed all 3-quote sequences to 2-quote (correct RFC 4180 encoding) across 30 Check formula rows. Each literal double-quote in the formula is now encoded as 2-quote inside the CSV quoted field.

Description: Screen flash when typing in UW Inputs for the first time after workbook open
RootCause: Two causes: (1) RunHealthCheckOnOpen runs LoadAllConfig (thousands of cell reads) and RunHealthCheck (writes to ErrorLog with formatting) without suppressing ScreenUpdating. (2) CreateFormulaTabs unconditionally sets Application.ScreenUpdating=True at all exit points. When called from pipeline via RefreshFormulaTabs (which expects ScreenUpdating=False), this prematurely enables screen updates. Then Application.CalculateFull runs visibly causing a flash.
Fix: (1) Added ScreenUpdating save/restore to RunHealthCheckOnOpen in KernelHealth.bas. (2) Changed CreateFormulaTabs in KernelFormula.bas to save ScreenUpdating state on entry and restore it at all 3 exit points (early-exit, normal-exit, ErrHandler) instead of unconditionally setting True.

Description: Losses and counts all zeros in Detail tab -- DevelopLosses skips all exposure months
RootCause: Reset() cleared m_devEnd(p)=0 for all programs. DevelopLosses checks If age > m_devEnd(p) which is always True (age >= 1 > 0), so every exposure month is skipped. m_devEnd was set correctly by LoadCurveParams in Initialize, but Reset runs after Initialize and before Execute in the pipeline.
Fix: Removed m_devEnd(p) = 0 from Reset(). m_devEnd is a curve parameter set in LoadCurveParams (part of Initialize), not a computation value that should be cleared between runs.

Description: Curve library config not found -- 80x W-852 warnings, all curves use defaults
RootCause: FindSectionStart in KernelConfigLoader scans rows 1-500 for config markers. formula_tab_config.csv grew to 1218 lines (golden test data), pushing CURVE_LIBRARY_CONFIG marker past row 500. Also GetCurveLibraryField had col<=10 bounds check instead of col<=16 for new anchor columns.
Fix: Changed FindSectionStart to use dynamic last row (ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count,1).End(xlUp).Row) instead of hardcoded 500. Changed GetCurveLibraryField bounds check from col<=10 to col<=16.

Description: E-222 Cannot parse DerivationRule for identity copy columns (C_ClsCt etc.)
RootCause: ComputeDerivedFields parser only supports A op B format. Ceded count columns (C_ClsCt, C_OpenCt, C_RptCt, C_UltCt) use single-column identity rules like G_ClsCt to copy gross counts to ceded.
Fix: Added identity/copy rule support to ComputeDerivedFields: if ParseDerivationRule fails, check if rule is a valid column name and copy values directly. Ceded counts now correctly equal gross counts.

Description: E-120 Input parameter not found: Attributes.EntityName (40x errors)
RootCause: Kernel modules (KernelOutput, KernelTabs, KernelProveIt) call InputValue(Attributes,EntityName,idx) for Summary/Charts/Exhibits. Insurance input_schema lacked this parameter. DetectEntityCount had a MaxEntities fallback but entity name retrieval did not.
Fix: Added Attributes.EntityName to insurance input_schema.csv (row 3). Added code in InsuranceDomainEngine.Initialize() to write program names from UW Inputs to the Inputs tab at row 3 for kernel consumption.

Description: Runtime Error 9 on Sheets(TAB_INPUTS) -- insurance model uses Assumptions tab
RootCause: BUG-062 fix hardcoded TAB_INPUTS (=Inputs) but insurance tab_registry names the kernel input tab Assumptions (configured via InputsTabName setting in granularity_config). KernelConfig.InputValue also hardcoded TAB_INPUTS.
Fix: Added KernelConfig.GetInputsTabName() helper that resolves InputsTabName setting with TAB_INPUTS fallback. Updated InputValue and InsuranceDomainEngine.Initialize() to use it.

Description: Named ranges point to wrong cells -- CTRL_TaxRate at C40 instead of C12
RootCause: CreateNamedRanges SINGLE branch builds reference with row-relative addressing ($C12 instead of $C$12). When cellAddr is provided without RowID, code does $+UCase(cellAddr) which misses the $ before the row number. Row shifts based on active cell context at creation time.
Fix: Changed the direct cellAddr branch to use ws.Range(cellAddr).Address(True, True) which returns a fully absolute reference ($C$12). All 16 SINGLE-type named ranges affected.

Description: Premium never fully earns -- EP only reaches ~92% of WP
RootCause: EarnPremium mid-month window was [m, m+term-1] (term months). First and last months each get 1/(2*term) but only term-2 middle months get 1/term. Fracs sum to (term-1)/term not 1.0. For term=12: 22/24=91.67%. Correct window is [m, m+term] (term+1 months): first half + (term-1) full middles + spill-over half = 1.0.
Fix: Changed loop from m+termMo-1 to m+termMo. Changed last-month check from ew=m+termMo-1 to ew=m+termMo. Removed m_horizon write guard (arrays sized to MAX_HORIZON=120 with Exit For guard). All EP-derived metrics (G_EComm, G_EFrontFee, C_EP, C_EComm) inherit the fix since they use m_epMon.

Description: Screen flash when typing in UW Inputs tab
RootCause: Merged cells (ColSpan=25) in frozen rows 1-2 span across the freeze pane column boundary at column C (col 3). Excel redraws frozen/unfrozen pane regions on every cell selection or edit when a merged cell straddles the boundary. UW Inputs has 7 cross-boundary merges (ColSpan 9-25). Freeze was set at (3, QS_DATA_START_COL=3) for all formula tabs regardless of layout type.
Fix: Changed KernelFormula.CreateFormulaTabs freeze logic: non-quarterly tabs (like UW Inputs) freeze at (3,1) for row-only freeze with no column boundary. Quarterly tabs (like UW Exec Summary) retain column freeze at (3, QS_DATA_START_COL) since they need horizontal scrolling and have no user-editable cells.

Description: FATAL crash at step 6: Column not found Period
RootCause: ColIndex returns -1 on failure (not 0) and logs SEV_FATAL. All 6 Period/CalPeriod fallback checks used '= 0' so fallback never triggered. ColLetter(-1) then raised a runtime error crashing the pipeline at WriteSummaryFormulas.
Fix: Added TryColIndex to KernelConfig -- silent probe that returns -1 without logging. Updated all 6 fallback locations in KernelOutput, KernelCompare, KernelFormula, KernelTabs (x3) to use TryColIndex for probe, ColIndex for the fallback (which logs FATAL only if both names fail).

Description: Balance columns stored as EOP but declared Incremental -- identity Sum(Paid)+Sum(CaseRsv)+Sum(IBNR)=Sum(Ult) broken
RootCause: WriteOutputs wrote Balance columns (CaseRsv, IBNR, Unpaid, OpenCt, IBNRCt, UnclosedCt) as end-of-period balances but column_registry FieldClass=Incremental. Sum of incremental paid + EOP reserves != ultimate. Identity only holds when all values are true incremental.
Fix: Converted all Balance columns to true incremental (change-in-EOP = EOP(cm)-EOP(cm-1)). Added tail closure row per program that forces ITD reserves=0, paid=ultimate, counts closed. Split AggregateToQuarterly into Ins_QuarterlyAgg.bas (AD-09). Added Inc() DRY helper. QuarterlySummary SUMIFS uses <=lastMon to reconstruct EOP from incremental.

Description: E-120 errors: Attributes.EntityName and Model Identity.ScenarioName not found in insurance config
RootCause: config_insurance/input_schema.csv was missing Attributes.EntityName (row 3, used by KernelOutput, KernelTabs, KernelProveIt for entity name lookup) and Model Identity.ScenarioName (row 7, used by Ins_GranularCSV for CSV filename). InsuranceDomainEngine.Initialize writes entity names to row 3 but InputValue could not resolve the row because the parameter was not in the schema.
Fix: Added Attributes.EntityName (row 3) and Model Identity.ScenarioName (row 7) to config_insurance/input_schema.csv. InputValue can now resolve both parameters to the correct Assumptions tab rows.

Description: W-811 warnings: QS_N_UNPAID_TOTAL and QS_N_ULT_TOTAL not found on QuarterlySummary
RootCause: KernelFormula.ResolveRowID capped its search at 500 rows. With 30 Incremental + 18 Derived metric sections at 12-13 rows each (10 entities per section), the QuarterlySummary tab has ~600 rows. N_Unpaid total lands at ~row 505 and N_Ult at ~row 518, both past the cap.
Fix: Increased ResolveRowID row cap from 500 to 2000 (matching FindRowIDInSheet in Ins_QuarterlyAgg.bas).

Description: Safe Mode prompt on next Excel launch after Setup
RootCause: Bootstrap kills Excel via Stop-Process -Force to protect XLSTART/Personal.xlsb (BUG-045). Excel writes a crash sentinel to HKCU Resiliency\StartupItems on launch and removes it on graceful exit. Process kill leaves the sentinel, so the next normal Excel launch detects it and offers Safe Mode.
Fix: Added StartupItems cleanup to the Bootstrap.ps1 finally block: after killing the process, remove all entries under Resiliency\StartupItems for Office 16.0 and 15.0. Follows the same pattern as DocumentRecovery cleanup (BUG-047).

Description: Negative Net Earned Premium in Q1 Y1 -- XOL premium added to QS ceded premium
RootCause: InsuranceDomainEngine.WriteOutputs added xolMon (monthly XOL cost $58,333) to C_WP and C_EP: outputs(row,cCWP)=gWP*cedePct+xolMon. XOL is non-proportional reinsurance and should not be part of quota-share ceded premium. In month 1, GEP~$69K but CEP~$79K (including XOL), making NEP negative.
Fix: Removed + xolMon from C_WP and C_EP lines in WriteOutputs. Ceded premium is now purely proportional (gWP*cedePct / gEP*cedePct). XOL data remains in engine arrays (m_xolCat, m_xolOther) for future use.

Description: Capital Activity header in wrong row and Amount Raised shows as header text
RootCause: Header row scanner in KernelFormula only scanned for Formula cellType when finding the first quarterly data row. Capital Activity has Input cells with Col=C (CAP_EQ_AMT at row 6) before the first Formula (CAP_EQ_CUMUL at row 7). Scanner found row 7, placed headers at row 6, overwriting the Amount Raised Input values with quarterly header labels. Also, quarterly Input cells were not replicated across quarterly columns.
Fix: Extended header row scanner to include Input cells with non-numeric Col (quarterly Input cells). Added quarterly Input replication: Input cells with Col=C on quarterly tabs now replicate defaults across all quarterly columns with format and font color, matching Formula cell replication pattern.

Description: Snapshot save crashes -- multiple robustness issues in KernelSnapshot
RootCause: Six issues: (1) GetProjectRoot crashes on unsaved workbook (InStrRev returns 0, Left(path,-1) error). (2) ActiveWorkbook.PrecisionAsDisplayed in BuildExecutionFingerprint violates AP-04. (3) No column count/headers in manifest -- cannot validate on load after config changes. (4) ImportDetailFromCsv uses current config column count, silently truncating snapshot data if column count changed. (5) Export subs (Detail, Inputs, ErrorLog, Settings) have no error handling -- file handles leak on error. (6) EnsureDirectoryExists has no error handling for MkDir failures.
Fix: (1) Added guards in GetProjectRoot for empty path and root-level paths with clear error messages. (2) Changed ActiveWorkbook to ThisWorkbook. (3) Added columnCount, columnHeaders array, entityCount, timeHorizon to manifest.json. (4) ImportDetailFromCsv now validates CSV column count vs config, warns on mismatch, uses larger count to avoid truncation, and restores CSV headers. (5) Added On Error GoTo handlers with file handle cleanup to all 4 export subs. (6) Added error handling to EnsureDirectoryExists with descriptive error message.

Description: Formula tabs fail with Type mismatch on bootstrap (E-800)
RootCause: ResolveRowID reads column A of referenced tabs into a Variant via Range.Value. When the tab has 0-1 rows of data (e.g. QuarterlySummary which is kernel-generated and empty during bootstrap), Range.Value returns a scalar, not a 2D array. Indexing a scalar as colData(r,1) causes VBA Error 13 Type mismatch. Affects both sample and insurance configs since both reference QuarterlySummary which is not a formula tab.
Fix: Ensured the range read always spans at least 2 rows (readToRow = max(lastRow,2)) so VBA always returns a 2D array. The extra empty row is harmless -- skipped by the Len(cellVal)>0 check.

Description: Formula tab annual total columns incomplete for quarterly Input and Balance cells
RootCause: KernelFormula.bas writes quarterly Input values across Q1-Q4 but leaves the annual total column blank. For Balance formulas using {PREV_Q:self}, the annual column blindly resolves the rolling formula which references blank input cells. For Flow formulas using cross-ref {PREV_Q:}, the annual column shows only Q4-based value instead of annual total.
Fix: Three fixes: (1) Quarterly Input cells now write =SUM(Q1:Q4) in annual column with grey shading and format. (2) Self-referencing {PREV_Q:} formulas (Balance) use =Q4_col (EOP) in annual column. (3) Cross-referencing {PREV_Q:} formulas without IFERROR (Flow) use =SUM(Q1:Q4) in annual column. Formulas without {PREV_Q:} resolve at annual column (current behavior preserved for ratios and row references).

Description: QuarterlySummary missing formulas for single-operand derived columns
RootCause: Ins_QuarterlyAgg.AggregateToQuarterly uses ParseSimpleRule to parse derivation rules. Single-operand copy rules (e.g. C_ClsCt='G_ClsCt') have no operator, so ParseSimpleRule returns False and the metric section is skipped entirely. This cascades: Net derived columns like N_ClsCt='G_ClsCt - C_ClsCt' cannot find the C_ClsCt total row, so their formulas also fail silently. Affects C_ClsCt, C_OpenCt, C_RptCt, C_UltCt, C_IBNRCt, C_UnclosedCt and all corresponding Net columns.
Fix: Added isCopyRule branch: when ParseSimpleRule fails but the rule is a valid column name (ColIndex > 0), treat as single-operand copy. Copy formulas reference the source column's rows directly (=<col><sourceRow>). All entity rows, total rows, annual totals, and tail columns handled for both copy and two-operand rules.

Description: CFS {PREV_Q:} resolves to annual total column instead of Q4 of prior year
RootCause: ResolveFormulaPlaceholders resolves {PREV_Q:RowID} as col-1. The quarterly column layout has annual total columns every 5th position (Q1,Q2,Q3,Q4,YearTotal). At Q1 of years 2-5 (e.g. col H=8), col-1 is the annual total column (col G=7), not Q4 of the previous year (col F=6). CFS change-in-balance formulas (like Change in Loss Reserves) incorrectly compute Q1Y2 minus Y1Total instead of Q1Y2 minus Q4Y1.
Fix: Added annual total column detection in {PREV_Q:} resolution. If col-1 falls on an annual total column ((col-1-QS_DATA_START_COL) Mod QS_COLS_PER_YEAR = QS_QUARTERS_PER_YEAR), skip back one more to reach Q4 of the prior year. Affects all formula tabs using {PREV_Q:} placeholders.

Description: FinancialSummary tab created for insurance config despite not being registered
RootCause: KernelBootstrap.bas had hardcoded EnsureSheet TAB_FINANCIAL_SUMMARY in the required-tabs section. FinancialSummary is a sample-domain formula tab, not a required kernel tab. For insurance config (which has its own FM tabs), this creates an empty orphan tab.
Fix: Removed the hardcoded EnsureSheet TAB_FINANCIAL_SUMMARY call from KernelBootstrap. Tab is already created via tab_registry loop when present in config_sample/tab_registry.csv. Insurance config has no tab_registry entry, so the tab is correctly not created.

Description: Excel crashes on workbook open -- OFFICE_MODULE_VERSION_MISMATCH between PERSONAL.XLSB and RDK VBA project
RootCause: Root cause: Office 16.0.19822.20086 has internal DLL version mismatch (pending update to 20114). Loading two VBA projects simultaneously (PERSONAL.XLSB from XLSTART + RDK's 30-module project) triggers access violation (0xc0000005) in EXCEL.EXE. Single VBA project loads fine. Contributing factors: (1) Bootstrap.ps1 force-kill left crash sentinels. (2) AP-06 em dash in InsuranceDomainEngine.bas. (3) Workbook_Open health check created COM objects during open.
Fix: Six fixes: (1) OpenRDK.cmd launcher temporarily disables PERSONAL.XLSB during Excel startup then restores it -- workaround until Office update. (2) Bootstrap.ps1 switched to graceful $excel.Quit(). (3) Bootstrap.ps1 cleans AutoRecover files. (4) Fixed AP-06 em dash. (5) Removed InjectWorkbookOpen. (6) Added FullExcelClean.ps1 and CleanResiliency.ps1. Permanent fix: apply Office update 20086->20114.

Description: Tail column generated on all quarterly formula tabs instead of UW Exec Summary only
RootCause: KernelFormula.bas generated tail columns (development beyond writing horizon) on every quarterly formula tab that had a grand total column. Tail data is only meaningful on UW Exec Summary; other tabs (Income Statement, CFS, Expense Summary) should not show it.
Fix: Added wantsTail flag at tab level: True only when tabName='UW Exec Summary'. Gate tail formula generation (qsDataYears > numYears And wantsTail) and tail header generation (writeTail parameter on WriteQuarterlyHeaders). Grand total column still generated for all qualifying tabs.

Description: Safe mode prompt when user double-clicks RDK_Model.xlsm after Setup -- UX gap plus stuck PERSONAL.XLSB
RootCause: Two issues: (1) Setup.bat prints 'Use OpenRDK.cmd' but users naturally double-click the .xlsm. Office 16.0.19822.20104 still has the dual-VBA-project DLL mismatch (BUG-081). Double-click loads PERSONAL.XLSB alongside RDK's 30-module project, causing access violation and safe mode sentinel. (2) OpenRDK.cmd renamed PERSONAL.XLSB to .rdk_bypass but lacked interrupt protection -- if interrupted (Ctrl+C, window close), PERSONAL.XLSB stays stuck renamed.
Fix: Four fixes: (1) Setup.bat now prompts 'Open workbook now? [Y/n]' and calls OpenRDK.cmd directly. (2) OpenRDK.cmd restores stuck .rdk_bypass from prior interrupted runs. (3) Bootstrap.ps1 conditional module import (PT-031) reduces VBA project from 30 to 27 modules for sample config. (4) OpenRDK.cmd verifies rename succeeded and prints manual restore instructions if it fails.

Description: Excel crash persists after conditional import -- corrupt COM stubs and startup overload
RootCause: Conditional import (BUG-082 fix 3) reduced modules from 30 to 27 but crash persisted. Diagnosis found 4 corrupt HKCU COM add-in stubs (PDFMaker, SNL, SPGMI, VS15ExcelAdaptor) with no Manifest/FriendlyName overriding proper HKLM registrations. PDFMaker stub changed LoadBehavior from 2 (demand) to 3 (startup), adding an extra startup load. Combined with S&P Cap IQ (2 COM add-ins + 3 XLA/UDF add-ins) + PERSONAL.XLSB + RDK's 27-module project = 8 items competing for Excel startup. Total startup load exceeded Excel's stability threshold.
Fix: Three root-cause fixes (resolved -- double-click works): (1) Moved PERSONAL.XLSB from XLSTART to PERSONAL_BACKUP (eliminates dual-VBA-project conflict). (2) Created CleanComStubs.ps1 to remove incomplete HKCU stubs that override HKLM registrations. (3) Bootstrap.ps1 finally block already cleans stubs post-run. OpenRDK.cmd workaround removed after root-cause fixes proved sufficient.

Description: Ceded count columns include CAT claims -- should be QS-subject (Attr+Seas) only
RootCause: column_registry.csv defined C_ClsCt, C_OpenCt, C_RptCt, C_UltCt, C_IBNRCt, C_UnclosedCt as Derived with copy rules (e.g. C_ClsCt=G_ClsCt). The kernel copied 100% of gross counts (including CAT layer) to ceded, regardless of QS treaty scope. This made N_ClsCt = G_ClsCt - C_ClsCt = 0 always, and net count columns showed zero claims even though CAT claims should be net-retained.
Fix: Changed 6 ceded count columns from Derived to Incremental in config_insurance/column_registry.csv. InsuranceDomainEngine.WriteOutputs now writes ceded counts directly from m_qsCls, m_qsRpt, m_qsCntUlt arrays (which exclude CAT per lyr<=2 filter in DevelopLosses). Flow counts use Inc() helper; Balance counts use EOP(cm)-EOP(cm-1) pattern. Tail closure row closes QS-subject open counts only.

Description: INV_Q_POOL uses gross reserves and gross UPR -- overstates investable pool by ceded portion
RootCause: Investable pool formula referenced UWEX_GRSV + UWEX_GUPR. Under QS cession the carrier does not invest the ceded float -- the reinsurer does. Pool should use net values.
Fix: Changed INV_Q_POOL to use UWEX_NRSV + UWEX_NUPR. Added UWEX_NUPR row to UW Exec Summary (= Gross UPR - Ceded UPR). Added UWEX_Q_NUPR named range.

Description: Save Snapshot fails on insurance config -- Subscript out of range on Inputs tab
RootCause: KernelSnapshot.ExportInputsToFile, ImportInputsFromCsv, and DetectEntityCount all reference TAB_INPUTS (=Inputs). Insurance config renamed this tab to Assumptions in tab_registry.csv. The Inputs tab does not exist in the insurance workbook, causing Error 9 (Subscript out of range) which surfaces as E-899 Save error.
Fix: Added GetInputsSheet() helper to KernelSnapshot.bas that tries TAB_INPUTS first, falls back to TAB_ASSUMPTIONS. Replaced all 3 hardcoded ThisWorkbook.Sheets(TAB_INPUTS) calls with GetInputsSheet().

Description: Investments liquidity floor ignored when floor exceeds allocation-based liquid amount
RootCause: INV_Q_INVESTED formula was MAX(Pool*InvestPct, Pool-Floor). When Floor was large, Pool-Floor went negative and MAX picked Pool*InvestPct, making Liquid=Pool*LiquidPct which ignored the floor entirely. Invested assets were over-allocated at the expense of the liquidity requirement.
Fix: Reversed solve order: Liquid Cash = MIN(Pool, MAX(Floor, Pool*LiquidAlloc%)) computed first, then Invested = MAX(0, Pool-Liquid). Floor is always respected. MIN(Pool,...) cap ensures Liquid+Invested=Pool identity holds even when Floor>Pool.

Description: Expense Summary Grand Total column produces garbage for COLUMN()-based growth formulas
RootCause: KernelFormula.bas Grand Total logic had two branches: (1) has {REF:} and NOT {ROWID:} -> SUM of annual totals, (2) Else -> resolve formula at GT column. COLUMN()-based formulas (no placeholders) fell into branch 2, making COLUMN() return a GT column index that computed a hypothetical Y6 quarter instead of summing actuals.
Fix: Split Else branch into three: (a) has {ROWID:}/{PREV_Q:} -> resolve at GT column (ratios/cumulative), (b) pure {REF:} or no placeholders -> SUM of annual totals. COLUMN()-based formulas now correctly sum.

Description: Balance Sheet check not zero -- investable pool proxy formula missed multiple BS items
RootCause: INV_Q_POOL was NRSV + NUPR + CAP_EQ_CUMUL, a proxy that excluded debt, tax payable, retained earnings, and other liabilities. This meant Cash+Invested did not equal Total L&E minus non-cash assets, causing BS_CHECK = Total_A - Total_LE to be nonzero.
Fix: Two-part fix: (1) Added BS_CUPR (Ceded UPR) as a BS asset row; (2) Changed INV_Q_POOL to BS-derived residual = BS_TOTAL_LE - BS_AR - BS_RI_RECV - BS_CUPR - BS_OTHER_A, guaranteeing balance by construction. Circular reference eliminated separately in BUG-090.

Description: BS-derived investable pool creates circular reference requiring Application.Iteration
RootCause: INV_Q_POOL references BS_TOTAL_LE which includes RE which includes Net Income which includes Investment Income which depends on Pool. AVERAGE-based income formulas (AVERAGE(prev,current)*yield/4) reference current-quarter balances, completing the circle. Application.Iteration worked but caused user confusion and slower recalc.
Fix: Changed all income and interest formulas from AVERAGE(prev,current) to BOQ (prior quarter only): INV_Q_INC_INV, INV_Q_INC_LIQ, CAP_SN_INT, CAP_OD_INT. Prior-quarter values are already determined so no circularity. Q1 income/interest = 0. Removed Application.Iteration from KernelBootstrap.bas.

Description: Grand Total column empty for Input rows on quarterly tabs with GrandTotal=TRUE
RootCause: KernelFormula.bas INPUT case block had annual total SUM handling but no Grand Total column handling. The Grand Total SUM-of-annual-totals logic was only in the FORMULA case block. Input rows on ORD and SWI tabs had empty GT cells, causing Formula total rows referencing them to evaluate to 0.
Fix: Added Grand Total column handling to the INPUT block in KernelFormula.bas: SUM of annual total columns, matching the Formula block pattern. Uses unique variable names (inGtCol, inGtFormula, inGtYr, inAnnTotCol) to avoid VBA scope collisions.

Description: CFS cash reconciliation nonzero: CFS_E_POOL double-counts invested assets
RootCause: CFS_E_POOL was defined as BS_CASH + BS_INVEST (total pool), but CFI already included the change in invested assets. BOQ started from Cash+Invest, then CFI subtracted delta-Invest again, so the reconciliation check (EOQ - Pool) was nonzero by Invest(t-1) - Invest(t).
Fix: Renamed CFS_E_POOL to CFS_E_CASH, changed formula from BS_CASH+BS_INVEST to BS_CASH only. CFS now reconciles to cash, with investment movements properly captured in CFI. Updated CFS_BOQ_CASH and CFS_CHECK to reference CFS_E_CASH.

Description: CFS cash reconciliation nonzero: interest expense double-counted in CFO and CFF
RootCause: IS_NETINC already includes interest expense (IS_PRETAX = OpInc - IntExp). CFS_INT_PAID in CFF subtracted interest a second time. Interest reduced cash twice: once through NI in CFO, once explicitly in CFF.
Fix: Removed CFS_INT_PAID from CFF section. Interest expense flows naturally through NI into CFO. Updated CFS_CFF formula to EQ_RAISE + DEBT_NET only. Consistent with US GAAP indirect method where interest is an operating item.

Description: CFS cash reconciliation nonzero: missing Ceded UPR (Prepaid Reinsurance) working capital adjustment
RootCause: BS_CUPR (Ceded UPR / Prepaid Reinsurance) is a balance sheet asset that changes period-over-period, but had no corresponding CFS working capital adjustment in CFO. Changes in this asset were unaccounted for in the cash flow statement.
Fix: Added CFS_D_CUPR in CFO section (Change in Prepaid Reinsurance = PREV_Q - current, asset sign convention) and CFS_E_CUPR echo in Supporting Calculations (= BS_CUPR). Added CFS_D_CUPR to CFS_CFO total formula.

Description: UWEX_GUPR and UWEX_NUPR compute quarterly change instead of ITD balance
RootCause: UWEX_GUPR was GWP-GEP (quarterly delta) and UWEX_NUPR was GUPR-(CWP-CEP) (also quarterly delta). Both should be cumulative end-of-quarter balances since they feed BS_UPR and BS_CUPR which are balance sheet items. Without prior-quarter carry-forward, the BS showed only QTD change, not a true UPR balance.
Fix: Changed UWEX_GUPR to PREV_Q(UWEX_GUPR)+GWP-GEP and UWEX_NUPR to PREV_Q(UWEX_NUPR)+NWP-NEP. Both now accumulate from prior quarter. Q1 starts from 0 (PREV_Q returns 0 for first period). BS_UPR and BS_CUPR now correctly show ITD balances.

Description: Quarterly header detection includes static Formula cells, placing headers in wrong row
RootCause: KernelFormula.bas header row detection (line 506) treated ALL Formula cells as quarterly regardless of Col value. Only Input cells required non-numeric Col check. Tabs with static Formula cells (Staffing Expense, Other Expense Detail, Sales Funnel, Investments) got headers placed at the first static Formula row instead of the first quarterly (Col=C) row. On Staffing Expense, this overwrote row 11 (HC Other) data with header text, causing #VALUE cascade through EXP -> IS -> BS -> INV.
Fix: Applied non-numeric Col check to both Formula and Input cell types in isQtrCell condition. Now only cells with Col=C are counted as quarterly for header placement. Fixes 4 affected tabs.

Description: Column A hidden on system tabs (ErrorLog, Config, etc.) that should retain visible column A
RootCause: AutoFitAllOutputTabs column A hiding loop iterated ALL worksheets including hidden system tabs. Hidden tabs (Config, ErrorLog, TestResults, Summary, ProveIt, Exhibits, Charts, CumulativeView, Analysis) should not have column A hidden since they may use it for data or debugging.
Fix: Added ws.Visible = xlSheetVisible check to column A hiding loop so only user-visible tabs get column A hidden.

Description: Missing Premiums Payable (Reinsurance) liability on Balance Sheet drains investable pool
RootCause: BS_AR = GWP*0.5 creates premium receivable as an asset deducted from investable pool, but BS_AP = 0 had no offsetting liability for premiums payable to reinsurers. This caused pool to be drained by GROSS written premium lag rather than NET, shrinking investable pool and cash even after profitable quarters.
Fix: Changed BS_AP from =0 to =-UWEX_CWP*0.5 (positive liability since UWEX_CWP is negative after PD-05). Pool drag is now net written premium * 0.5 instead of gross. CFS working capital (CFS_D_AP) automatically picks up the change. Renamed label to Premiums Payable (Reinsurance).

Description: Y5 Written Premium truncated on UW Exec Summary - short-tail programs output zero for months 49-60
RootCause: WriteOutputs loop bounded by m_devEnd(p) which is set from curve MaxAge. Property programs with MaxAge=36 had m_devEnd=36, so months 37-60 were never written to the Detail tab. SpreadPremium correctly computed all 60 months of premium, but WriteOutputs truncated output at pDevEnd. Result: Y5 WP showed 141m instead of 296m (only long-tail programs contributed Y5 data).
Fix: Added floor check after curve MaxAge computation: If maxDevEnd < m_horizon Then maxDevEnd = m_horizon. This ensures all premium months (1 to m_horizon) are always output regardless of curve development length. Loss development beyond horizon still works as before for long-tail programs.

Description: BUG-099 fix ineffective: m_horizon=0 when LoadCurveParams runs during Initialize
RootCause: m_horizon is set in Execute() (line 306) but LoadCurveParams runs during Initialize() (line 127). At Initialize time m_horizon=0 (VBA default), so the BUG-099 floor check (If maxDevEnd < m_horizon) is a no-op. Property programs with short curves (MaxAge<49) still get devEnd<49, producing no Y5 Detail rows. SUMIFS on QuarterlySummary returns 0 for those programs in Y5. Only Casualty programs (MaxAge>=120) showed Y5 data.
Fix: Set m_horizon from KernelConfig.GetTimeHorizon() at the start of Initialize(), before ReadUWInputs and LoadCurveParams. Now devEnd floor check in LoadCurveParams sees m_horizon=60 and correctly enforces devEnd>=60 for all programs.

Description: Tail column on UW Exec Summary loses PD-05 negation prefix for ceded cross-tab references
RootCause: KernelFormula.bas line 452 hand-crafted the Tail formula as ='Tab'!cell, discarding the content prefix/suffix. For content like =-{REF:QuarterlySummary!QS_CQ_WP_TOTAL}, the =- negation was lost. Quarterly columns used ResolveFormulaPlaceholders which preserved the prefix. Affected 5 rows: UWEX_CWP, UWEX_CEP, UWEX_CLLAE, UWEX_CEDCOMM, UWEX_GFFE. Their Tail values showed positive instead of negative.
Fix: Replaced hard-coded ='Tab'!cell with prefix-preserving construction: extract Left(content, refStart-1) as prefix and Mid(content, refEnd+1) as suffix, reconstruct as prefix + quotedTab + ! + col + row + suffix. Matches ResolveFormulaPlaceholders behavior. Also handles tab names with spaces (conditional quoting).

Description: Staffing Expense annual/GT totals wrong: multiplicative formula resolved with summed operands
RootCause: KernelFormula.bas annual total Else branch resolved all non-{PREV_Q:} {ROWID:} formulas at the annual column position. For multiplicative formulas like HC*Sal/4*(1+BenefitsFactor), this multiplied summed operands (e.g. annual HC=4x, annual Sal=4x, annual Factor=4x0.30=1.20) producing grossly inflated totals. Same issue at Grand Total column. Only ratio (IFERROR) formulas need resolve-at-annual for correct weighted averages.
Fix: Split annual total and GT logic: IFERROR formulas still resolve at annual/GT column (correct weighted ratios). All other {ROWID:} formulas (additive and multiplicative) use SUM(Q1:Q4) for annual and SUM(annual totals) for GT. For additive formulas the result is identical; for multiplicative formulas it correctly sums already-computed quarterly values.

Description: Triangle SUMIFS matched $B (quarter label) instead of program name - all zeros
RootCause: SUMIFS entity criteria referenced $B{row} which contains Q1Y1 etc., but Detail EntityName column has program names like Base Case GL. Every SUMIFS returned 0 because no entity matched.
Fix: Write program name in hidden col A for each data row. SUMIFS now references $A{row} for entity match. Also moved Ult column under % header and added spacer column between dollar and % sections.

Description: Triangle SUMIFS cannot isolate exposure quarter cohorts from Detail tab
RootCause: Detail tab grain is (Program, CalendarMonth) with G_Paid aggregated across ALL exposure months. Triangle SUMIFS on CalPeriod range picks up loss development from prior exposure quarters mixed into later calendar months. Q2Y1 DQ1 showed 46k instead of ~10k because it included Q1Y1 exposure months developing at ages 3-6. SUMIFS approach is structurally incompatible with exposure-cohort triangles.
Fix: Reverted to VBA-computed values using per-exposure-month ultimates (m_ultMon) and direct curve evaluation (EvaluateCurve with DE-08 offset). Each triangle cell now correctly isolates its exposure quarter cohort by looping ep = epStart to epEnd and evaluating CDF at the correct development age. Dollar amounts and % of ultimate both computed in VBA. UW Exec Summary and QuarterlySummary confirmed correct (calendar-quarter financial view, not cohort view).

Description: Ultimates computed from EP instead of WP causing unequal exposure quarter cohorts
RootCause: ComputeUltimates used m_epMon (earned premium per calendar month) as the exposure base for m_ultMon. Due to mid-month earning assumption with 12-month terms, EP ramps up over months 1-13 then stabilizes. Q1Y1 EP=125k while Q1Y2 EP=1M, producing different ultimates (25k vs 200k) despite identical 1M written premium. Ultimates should not develop -- they should be locked at WP x ELR when premium is written.
Fix: Changed m_ultMon(p,lyr,ep) = m_wpMon(p,ep) * elr (was m_epMon). WP-based m_ultMon drives loss development curves and IBNR/Unpaid reserves. Added parallel EP-based arrays (m_cumEpUlt, m_qsEpUlt) for G_Ult and C_Ult on the Detail tab (financial statement view: loss = EP x ELR). Balance sheet reserves (IBNR, Unpaid) continue to use WP-based m_cumUlt (full exposure base). Tail closure row uses WP-based cumulative to force paid=ultimate. DE-10 locked.

Description: Subscript out of range in DevelopLosses: m_epUltMon accessed beyond MAX_HORIZON
RootCause: m_epUltMon and m_epMon are dimensioned (1 To 10, 1 To 120) matching MAX_HORIZON. DevelopLosses loops cm = 1 To devHorizon where devHorizon can reach MAX_DEV_ENDPOINT (480) for long-tail programs. When cm > 120, m_epUltMon(p, cm) causes subscript out of range. No new EP or EP-based ultimate exists beyond the writing horizon.
Fix: Added ElseIf cm <= m_horizon branch. For cm > m_horizon (run-off period), carry forward the prior cumulative value unchanged. Same fix applied to m_cumEP, m_cumEpUlt, and m_cumEpCntUlt.

Description: Ins_Tests crashes entire suite on first error: no per-group error handling
RootCause: RunInsuranceTests had On Error GoTo ErrHandler at the top but individual test subs (TestReserveIdentities, TestCrossTabReconciliation, etc.) had no error handling. Any CDbl on a formula cell returning an error value or any missing tab/row crashed the entire suite with Application-defined or object-defined error, logging E-380 and aborting all remaining tests.
Fix: Wrapped each test group call in On Error Resume Next with per-group error capture. If a group fails, the error is logged as a FAIL result and the next group runs. CDbl errors on formula cells are caught at the caller level rather than crashing the suite.

Description: Ins_Tests E-380 persists despite per-group error wrapping
RootCause: On Error GoTo ErrHandler was re-engaged at line 63 after the test group calls. Errors in the summary block (lines 66-73) or from On Error GoTo itself triggered the ErrHandler which logged E-380. The mixed GoTo/Resume Next pattern created unreliable error state.
Fix: Changed entire RunInsuranceTests to On Error Resume Next throughout. Removed all On Error GoTo ErrHandler transitions. Every operation is protected by Resume Next. Errors in individual test groups are caught and logged as FAIL results without triggering the global error handler.

Description: Ins_Tests references old Triangles tab name after rename to Loss Triangles
RootCause: TestTriangleCohortIsolation and TestTriangleOrdering referenced ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Triangles) but the tab was renamed to Loss Triangles. Both tests failed with tab not found, producing 2 FAIL results and triggering E-210 pipeline error.
Fix: Updated all 3 references from Triangles to Loss Triangles in Ins_Tests.bas.

Description: Curve library loader hardcoded 16 columns but schema expanded to 21
RootCause: LoadCurveLibraryConfig passed 16 to LoadSection2D but the 5-point anchor schema has 21 columns. Columns 17-21 (MaxAge_TL25 through Description) were never loaded. GetCurveLibraryField bounds check also capped at col<=16. Result: MaxAge interpolation used defaults (120) instead of calibrated values (240). Curve ordering tests may have failed due to incorrect parameter resolution.
Fix: Changed LoadSection2D column count from 16 to 21. Changed GetCurveLibraryField bounds check from col<=16 to col<=21.

Description: Balance sheet $2023 imbalance from dual EP/WP ultimate basis
RootCause: BUG-105 introduced EP-based G_Ult (m_cumEpUlt) for Detail tab while IBNR/Unpaid reserves used WP-based m_cumUlt. IS accumulated EP-based losses into Retained Earnings while BS Loss Reserve held WP-based reserves. During ramp-up EP lags WP so cumulative EP-ult < WP-ult. The difference ($2023) appeared as BS imbalance.
Fix: INITIAL FIX WAS WRONG: Reverted to WP-based which broke IS losses. Superseded by BUG-112.

Description: Calendar quarter IS losses wrongly computed from WP instead of EP after BUG-111
RootCause: BUG-111 reverted G_Ult to WP-based (m_cumUlt) to fix BS imbalance but this made Q1Y1 show Ult=200k (WP x ELR) instead of 25k (EP x ELR). Calendar quarter financials must show EP-based losses. The correct fix: make ALL Detail output metrics use EP-based cumulative so BS and IS are internally consistent.
Fix: Changed G_Ult, C_Ult, IBNR, Unpaid, and all count equivalents to EP-based (m_cumEpUlt). Changed tail closure to close to EP-based ultimate. Same for ceded (QS) block. WP-based m_cumUlt retained only for DevelopLosses curve accumulation. BS balances because IS losses and BS reserves both use EP-based ultimate.

Description: BS $2023 imbalance from EP spillover not captured in EP-based ultimate
RootCause: m_epUltMon was computed inside ComputeUltimates gated by If m_wpMon(p,ep)=0 Then Skip. For months where WP=0 but EP>0 (earning spillover from prior months with 12-month terms), the EP had no corresponding EP-based ultimate. m_cumEpUlt carried forward the incomplete total. With 60-month horizon and 12-month terms, months 61-72 have EP from WP written in months 49-60 but m_epUltMon was zero. This created a $2023 gap.
Fix: Moved m_epUltMon computation to a separate pass after all layers using blended ELR (total WP-ult / total WP). Applied to ALL months with EP including spillover. Expanded m_epUltMon array from 120 to 480. Removed m_horizon gate from cumulative EP-ult accumulation. QS EP-ult computed from gross EP-ult * QS ratio per calendar month.

Description: BS $2023 persists: devEnd too short to include EP spillover months on Detail
RootCause: BUG-113 computed m_epUltMon for spillover months 61-72 but devEnd was floored at m_horizon (60). DevelopLosses and WriteOutputs loop cm=1 to devEnd, so months 61-72 never made it to the Detail tab. Cumulative EP-ult at month 60 was missing the spillover. IS accumulated only through month 60 while BS UPR captured the full WP-EP gap. Mismatch persisted.
Fix: Changed devEnd floor from m_horizon to m_horizon + m_progTerm(p). For 12-month term programs, devEnd >= 72, ensuring all EP spillover months appear on the Detail tab. Also fixed m_epUltMon spillover range to use actual program term instead of hardcoded +12.

Description: E-800 NumberFormat error aborts all formula tab creation causing #REF cascade
RootCause: CreateFormulaTabs had On Error GoTo ErrHandler at the top. Any error in the config row processing loop (e.g. Unable to set NumberFormat on a merged or protected cell) jumped to ErrHandler which exited the entire sub. All tabs after the failing row got no formulas, causing #REF! on Investments, IS, BS, CFS. The specific NumberFormat error was pre-existing but became visible when the cascade broke downstream tests.
Fix: Added On Error Resume Next within the config row loop (per-row error recovery). Errors are logged as W-800 warnings with tab/rowID context and processing continues to the next row. On Error GoTo ErrHandler restored after the loop for non-row errors. One bad cell no longer kills all formula tabs.

Description: UW Program Detail Paid/CI 10x too high: WP-based development mixed with EP-based Ult
RootCause: DevelopLosses accumulated Paid and CI using WP-based m_ultMon * CDF but G_Ult used EP-based m_cumEpUlt. Q1Y1 showed Paid=101,744 (WP development on 1M) but Ult=25,000 (EP on 125k). IBNR = Ult-CI = 25k-191k = -166k (negative nonsense). All Detail metrics must use the same EP basis.
Fix: INITIAL FIX (epScale) was a band-aid that created inconsistency between CSV, Detail, and Triangles. Superseded by BUG-117.

Description: Root cause fix: m_ultMon changed from WP-based to EP-based, eliminating all band-aids
RootCause: Multiple band-aid fixes (BUG-105 through BUG-116) tried to reconcile WP-based m_ultMon with EP-based financial statements using epScale ratios, blendedELR, parallel EP arrays (m_cumEpUlt, m_qsEpUlt, m_epUltMon). This created inconsistencies between the CSV, Detail tab, and Triangles -- each computed development differently. The CSV showed WP-based values while Detail showed EP-scaled values.
Fix: Changed m_ultMon = m_epMon * ELR (was m_wpMon * ELR). EP is now the single source of truth. All development (Paid, CI, counts) = m_ultMon * CDF directly. No epScale, no parallel EP arrays, no blendedELR. CSV, Detail, QuarterlySummary, and Triangles all derive from the same m_ultMon * CDF computation. Removed m_cumEpUlt, m_qsEpUlt, m_epUltMon, m_epCntUltMon arrays and all associated code.

Description: Compile error: Type mismatch on Array() passed to RunExtensions ByRef outputs() As Variant
RootCause: PostBootstrap extension dispatch in KernelBootstrap passed Array() (empty Variant) as second arg to KernelExtension.RunExtensions which expects ByRef outputs() As Variant. VBA cannot coerce an empty Array() literal to a typed Variant array parameter.
Fix: Replaced Array() with a properly declared and ReDim-ed Variant array: Dim bootOutputs() As Variant / ReDim bootOutputs(0).

Description: Compile error: Label not defined (ErrHandler) in SetupDashboardTab
RootCause: Config-driven DashboardButton code in SetupDashboardTab used On Error GoTo ErrHandler after On Error Resume Next. ErrHandler is a label in BootstrapWorkbook, not in SetupDashboardTab. VBA compile error: Label not defined.
Fix: Changed On Error GoTo ErrHandler to On Error GoTo 0 (restore default error handling within SetupDashboardTab scope).

Description: Save/Load and Export Report buttons error: macro not found in KernelFormSetup
RootCause: button_config.csv referenced KernelFormSetup.ShowWorkspaceExplorer and KernelFormSetup.ShowReportExplorer but these functions are in KernelFormHelpers, not KernelFormSetup.
Fix: Fixed button_config.csv macro names: KernelFormSetup -> KernelFormHelpers.

Description: Regression test does not load golden inputs - compares current state only
RootCause: KernelWorkspace.ExportStateToFolder did not call KernelTabIO.ExportAllInputTabs. The input_tabs/ folder was never created in workspace versions. hasGoldenInputs check on line 373 returned False, so the regression test skipped loading golden inputs and re-running, and just compared the current Detail tab against the golden (which was different because current inputs differed).
Fix: Added KernelTabIO.ExportAllInputTabs verDir call to ExportStateToFolder in KernelWorkspace.bas. New saves now include input_tabs/ folder. Must re-save golden baseline for full regression to work.

Description: Compile error in CompareTabToCSV: duplicate Dim cStr + non-ASCII em-dash
RootCause: Two issues: (1) Added cStr2 variable but left original Dim cStr As String, causing duplicate variable in same scope. (2) Comment on line 504 had UTF-8 em-dash character (non-ASCII) that fails VBA import.
Fix: (1) Removed Dim cStr, reused cStr2 for string comparison. (2) Replaced em-dash with -- in comment.

Description: ScanForFormulaErrors false positive on clean model — all tabs flagged as having errors
RootCause: SpecialCells(xlCellTypeFormulas, xlErrors) throws Error 1004 when no error cells exist. Under On Error Resume Next, the error was swallowed but the subsequent For Each loop iterated undefined state, causing errCount to increment. Every tab was flagged.
Fix: Replaced For Each loop with Set errCells = SpecialCells(...) + Err.Number check. If Err.Number=0 and errCells is not Nothing, tab has errors. Err.Clear between tabs.

Description: LINT-12 CRLF error on KernelButtons.bas
RootCause: New file created with LF-only line endings by Write tool.
Fix: Applied sed CRLF conversion.

Description: 4 lint failures (CRLF + non-ASCII) are stale VBA module imports, not code bugs
RootCause: Lint reads from VBA project modules inside the workbook, not from .bas files on disk. The .bas files were fixed (CRLF + no non-ASCII) but the workbook still has the old imported versions. Re-running Setup.bat re-imports all .bas files and clears the lint errors.
Fix: No code fix. Re-run Setup.bat to re-import clean .bas files. Documented as stale-import artifact.

Description: Workspace version metadata (Last Run, Duration) empty in WorkspaceExplorer
RootCause: Existing workspace versions saved before the lastRunAt/runDuration fields were added to WriteManifestJson have no run metadata. ReadJsonField returns empty string for missing fields. Not a code bug -- data simply not present in old manifests. New saves will include the fields.
Fix: No code fix needed. Documented as expected behavior for pre-enhancement versions. Re-saving populates the fields.

Description: LINT-12 CRLF errors on DomainEngine.bas, Ins_Presentation.bas, KernelTabIO.bas
RootCause: Files created by Write tool had LF-only line endings. VBA import requires CRLF.
Fix: Applied sed CRLF conversion to all 3 files.

Description: Curve Reference tab shows identical values across all Trend Levels
RootCause: CurveRefPct UDF formulas with constant arguments (e.g., =CurveRefPct(Property,Paid,10,1)) not recalculating after bootstrap. All TLs show TL50 values. Config and interpolation code identical to working version. Suspected cause: VBA UDFs with all-constant arguments may cache results and not recalculate without explicit CalculateFull.
Fix: Added Application.CalculateFull at end of BootstrapWorkbook after ScreenUpdating restored. Forces all UDF formulas to evaluate with correct parameters.

Description: Compile error: ByRef argument type mismatch on btnHeight in CreateButtonsFromConfig
RootCause: CreateButtonsFromConfig declared btnHeight As Long but AddMacroButton expects btnHeight As Double. VBA cannot pass Long ByRef to Double parameter.
Fix: Changed Dim btnHeight As Long to Dim btnHeight As Double.

Description: Snapshot Load does not restore UW Inputs or any formula-driven input tab data
RootCause: ExportInputsToFile only exported parameters from input_schema (FederalTaxRate, ScenarioName). It did not capture UW Inputs, Capital Activity, Investments, Staffing Expense, or any other input tab data. LoadSnapshot restored only 2 values on Assumptions; all actual model inputs were unchanged.
Fix: Created KernelTabIO.bas module with ExportAllInputTabs/ImportAllInputTabs. These read tab_registry for Category=Input tabs, export each tab's used range as CSV to input_tabs/ subfolder, and restore via batch array write. Wired into SaveSnapshot, LoadSnapshot, and LoadSnapshotInputsOnly. Split from KernelSnapshot to stay under 64KB.

Description: Entity names overwrite FederalTaxRate on Assumptions tab after Assumptions cleanup
RootCause: InsuranceDomainEngine.Initialize line 141 wrote program names to row 3 of the input tab (GetInputsTabName=Assumptions). After removing EntityName from input_schema and moving FederalTaxRate to row 3, entity names overwrote the tax rate. Also GetEntityName fallback read row 3 of Assumptions (wrong tab entirely). Also DetectEntityCount used MaxEntities=10 blindly instead of counting actual programs.
Fix: (1) Removed BUG-062 entity name write from InsuranceDomainEngine.Initialize. (2) Added EntitySourceTab and EntitySourceRow to granularity_config (UW Inputs, row 6). (3) GetEntityName reads from EntitySourceTab/Row. (4) DetectEntityCount counts non-empty names from EntitySourceTab. (5) WriteRunMetadataTab uses EntitySourceTab for BU lookup.

Description: CTRL_FedTaxRate named range resolves to entity name instead of tax rate; #VALUE! cascade on Balance Sheet
RootCause: Removed EntityName from input_schema but GenerateInputsTab still wrote Entity -> header on row 3 of Assumptions tab. FederalTaxRate (input_schema Row=3) collided with entity names written to row 3 col C. CTRL_FedTaxRate named range (Assumptions!C3) resolved to Base Case GL instead of 0.21. IS_TAX formula =MAX(0,PRETAX*CTRL_FedTaxRate) got #VALUE!. Cascaded to BS retained earnings, equity, total L&E, balance check.
Fix: (1) GenerateInputsTab now checks hasEntityParam -- skips Entity -> header when no EntityName in input_schema. (2) Number format application limited to col C for global-only tabs. (3) PopulateSampleData takes hasEntities parameter -- skips entity name columns for global tabs. (4) CTRL_Inflation named range removed (GeneralInflation was dead code).

Description: Bootstrap crash at SetupSummaryStructure: Summary tab removed but step still called
RootCause: Removed Summary, Exhibits, Charts, CumulativeView, Analysis from FM tab_registry but SetupSummaryStructure unconditionally accessed ThisWorkbook.Sheets(TAB_SUMMARY). Subscript out of range.
Fix: Added existence guard in SetupSummaryStructure (If wsSummary Is Nothing Then Exit Sub). Also added same guard to KernelOutput.WriteSummaryFormulas. ProtectOutputTabs already had On Error Resume Next. KernelTabs references are safe because REFRESH_PRESENTATIONS pipeline step is disabled.

Description: Load Snapshot freezes Excel; Load Inputs Only does nothing visible
RootCause: Three issues: (1) LoadSnapshot called LoadAllConfig at top which reads Config sheet (stale), then after config restore copied CSVs to disk but called LoadAllConfig again which still reads stale Config sheet -- never reloaded from disk. (2) No ScreenUpdating=False/Calculation=Manual, so WriteSummaryFormulas triggered massive recalc with screen painting, freezing Excel. (3) No RefreshFormulaTabs after restore, so formula tabs showed old data. LoadSnapshotInputsOnly had the same stale-config issue plus missing ScreenUpdating control.
Fix: (1) Made LoadConfigFromDisk Public so KernelSnapshot can call it after restoring CSVs to disk. Restore sequence is now: copy CSVs to disk -> LoadConfigFromDisk (disk to Config sheet) -> LoadAllConfig (Config sheet to memory). (2) Added ScreenUpdating=False and Calculation=Manual at start of both Load functions, restored at end and in ErrHandler. (3) Added RefreshFormulaTabs call after data restore in LoadSnapshot so formula tabs recalculate against restored data.

Description: RBC R4 Business Risk shows GWP*factor in Q1 then 0 for all subsequent quarters
RootCause: R4 used {PREV_Q:RBC_GWP_PRIOR} for quarter-over-quarter growth. For startup with level premium, all growth is in Q1 (from 0), Q2+ shows 0 growth. Conceptually wrong: NAIC R4 is premium-based risk, not quarter-over-quarter delta. Also {PREV_Q:} Q1 resolution drops subtraction, leaving raw GWP without factor in edge cases.
Fix: Changed R4 formula from MAX(0,GWP-PREV_Q)*factor to GWP*factor. For a de novo carrier all premium is growth risk. Removed GWP_PRIOR echo row (no longer needed).

Description: RDK_Value UDFs return 0 -- cache never populated
RootCause: ComputeToCache dispatch called InsuranceDomainEngine_FormulaAPI.ComputeToCache (wrong module name). Also, Execute passed outputs array via Application.Run which cannot pass arrays ByRef (BUG-034 pattern).
Fix: Fixed dispatch to use FormulaAPIEntry branding config. Fixed Execute to use KernelEngine.DomainOutputs handoff pattern.

Description: Reconciliation always passes even with zeros
RootCause: ReconcileAgainstQS was a stub -- inner comparison loop was empty, always returned empty string (pass).
Fix: Implemented full reconciliation: scans UWEX tab rows, compares each quarterly cell to cached value, reports diffs with metric/quarter/expected/actual. $0.01 tolerance.

Description: UW Program Detail #REF! errors in formula config
RootCause: Per-program formulas referenced {REF:Quarterly Summary!QS_*} but QS tab is empty in formula-driven model (populated only on Generate Detail).
Fix: Converted 110 {REF:Quarterly Summary!} references to =RDK_Value() UDF calls. Added per-entity cache entries in Ins_FormulaAPI.AggregateToUWEXCache.

Description: RDK_Value UDFs show 0 after bootstrap -- cache marked ready despite empty
RootCause: RunDomainCompute fails silently (config not loaded, entity count 0, or validation fails in UDF context). m_cacheReady set to True regardless, preventing future retry. Also: bootstrap does not invalidate cache after config load.
Fix: (1) Only mark m_cacheReady=True if m_cache.Count>0. (2) Added config-readiness guard in ComputeToCache (exit if GetColumnCount=0). (3) Added KernelComputeCache.InvalidateCache call at end of bootstrap before CalculateFull.

Description: Setup option 3 hangs indefinitely during bootstrap
RootCause: Timer-based debounce (50ms) failed: each UDF computation takes >50ms, so every subsequent UDF cell triggers a full domain engine recomputation. 600+ cells x 5s each = hours. Re-entry guard using Boolean flag was needed instead.
Fix: Replaced timer-based debounce with m_computing Boolean re-entry guard. First RDK_Value call sets m_computing=True, runs computation, sets m_computing=False. All subsequent UDF calls during same calc cycle see m_computing=True, skip computation, read from cache. Input hash comparison still used for detecting changes after initial computation.

Description: Setup option 3 still hangs after re-entry guard fix
RootCause: ComputeInputHash() called inside RDK_Value for every UDF cell when cache is ready. Reads ~50 worksheet cells per call. 600 UDF cells x 50 reads = 30,000 worksheet reads per calc cycle just for hash comparison. Worksheet reads from UDF context are extremely slow in Excel.
Fix: Removed ComputeInputHash from UDF path entirely. Cache is now invalidated explicitly via InvalidateCache (called by Refresh Model button or bootstrap). UDFs only check m_cacheReady Boolean -- zero worksheet reads. Added RefreshModel button to Dashboard for input-change refresh.

Description: Setup option 3 still slow -- domain engine runs inside UDF context during CalculateFull
